_disclosure: This file is NOT part of the BetaNYC/New-York-City-Budget source data. Schedule C reconciliation reports only publish dollar amounts per named CATEGORY (e.g. 'CRIMINAL JUSTICE SERVICES', 'CULTURAL ORGANIZATIONS', 'HOMELESS SERVICES') - there is no 'sector' field anywhere in the source. The 5-bucket grouping used by this app's Historical Timeline chart (socialServices / youthEducation / artsCulture / healthWellness / environmentPublicSpace) is an editorial classification created for this app, mapping every real category name (across all 19 fiscal years and every naming variant/typo/rename BetaNYC's own filings used) into exactly one of the 5 buckets below. Every dollar amount that goes into a bucket is real and sourced from the category's `printed` value in the matching fyNN.json file in this same directory - only the grouping label is ours.
verification: For every fiscal year FY2009-FY2027, the 5 bucket sums computed from this mapping add up exactly to that year's real GRAND TOTAL (printed/adopted column) with zero residual - i.e. every category in every year is assigned to exactly one bucket, and no category is dropped or double-counted.
_speakerException: The 'SPEAKER'S INITIATIVE TO ADDRESS CITYWIDE NEEDS' entry below shows 'socialServices' only as the fallback used for FY2016-FY2019. For FY2020-FY2027 that category is NOT mapped by this table - it is a content-free catch-all, so its dollars are split across all 5 sectors by each underlying award's administering agency. See speaker-initiative-analysis.json in this directory for the agencyToSector map, the FY27 breakdown, and the full methodology.
